952	def save_to_gguf(
953	    model_type           : str,
954	    model_dtype          : str,
955	    is_sentencepiece     : bool = False,
956	    model_directory      : str = "unsloth_finetuned_model",
957	    quantization_method  = "fast_quantized", # Can be a list of options! ["q4_k_m", "q8_0", "q5_k_m"]
958	    first_conversion     : str = None,
959	    _run_installer = None, # Non blocking install of llama.cpp
960	):
961	    # logger.warning(
962	    #     "NOTICE: llama.cpp GGUF conversion is currently unstable, since llama.cpp is\n"\
963	    #     "undergoing some major bug fixes as at 5th of May 2024. This is not an Unsloth issue.\n"\
964	    #     "Please be patient - GGUF saving should still work, but might not work as well."
965	    # )
966	    assert(model_dtype == "float16" or model_dtype == "bfloat16")
967	    model_dtype = "f16" if model_dtype == "float16" else "bf16"
968	
969	    # Convert quantization_method to list
970	    if   isinstance(quantization_method, list):  pass
971	    elif isinstance(quantization_method, str):   quantization_method = [ quantization_method, ]
972	    elif isinstance(quantization_method, tuple): quantization_method = list(quantization_method)
973	    else:
974	        raise TypeError("Unsloth: quantization_method can only be a string or a list of strings")
975	    pass
976	
977	    # Check if bfloat16 is supported
978	    if model_dtype == "bf16" and not torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported():
979	        logger.warning(
980	            "Unsloth: Cannot convert to bf16 GGUF since your computer doesn't support it.\n"\
981	            "We shall switch instead to f16."
982	        )
983	        model_dtype = "f16"
984	    pass
985	
986	    # Check first_conversion as well
987	    if first_conversion is None:
988	        first_conversion = model_dtype
989	    pass
990	
991	    # Check I quants
992	    for quant_method in quantization_method:
993	        if quant_method.startswith("iq2"):
994	            raise RuntimeError("Unsloth: Currently iq2 type quantizations aren't supported yet - sorry!")
995	    pass
996	
997	    # Careful convert.py is only for Llama / Mistral based archs
998	    use_fast_convert = False
999	    if not is_sentencepiece:      use_fast_convert = False # Llama-3
1000	    elif model_type == "llama":   use_fast_convert = True
1001	    elif model_type == "mistral": use_fast_convert = True
1002	    pass
1003	    logger.warning_once(f"Unsloth: Converting {model_type} model. Can use fast conversion = {use_fast_convert}.")
1004	
1005	    # Map quant methods
1006	    new_quantization_method = []
1007	    for quant_method in quantization_method:
1008	        if   quant_method == "not_quantized":  quant_method = model_dtype
1009	        elif quant_method == "fast_quantized": quant_method = "q8_0"
1010	        elif quant_method == "quantized":      quant_method = "q4_k_m"
1011	        elif quant_method is None:             quant_method = "q8_0"
1012	
1013	        # Check if wrong method
1014	        if quant_method not in ALLOWED_QUANTS.keys():
1015	            error = f"Unsloth: Quant method = [{quant_method}] not supported. Choose from below:\n"
1016	            for key, value in ALLOWED_QUANTS.items():
1017	                error += f"[{key}] => {value}\n"
1018	            raise RuntimeError(error)
1019	        pass
1020	
1021	        new_quantization_method.append(quant_method)
1022	    pass
1023	    quantization_method = new_quantization_method
1024	
1025	    print_info = \
1026	        f"==((====))==  Unsloth: Conversion from QLoRA to GGUF information\n"\
1027	        f"   {chr(92)}{chr(92)}   /|    [0] Installing llama.cpp might take 3 minutes.\n"\
1028	        f"O^O/ {chr(92)}_/ {chr(92)}    [1] Converting HF to GGUF 16bits might take 3 minutes.\n"\
1029	        f"{chr(92)}        /    [2] Converting GGUF 16bits to {quantization_method} might take 10 minutes each.\n"\
1030	        f' "-____-"     In total, you will have to wait at least 16 minutes.\n'

2570	    import inspect

2572	    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

2574	    # And now re add our saving methods!

2587	    """

2595	    elif "tags" in arguments:

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass

94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass
174	pass

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass
276	
277	    @staticmethod
278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)
301	        d_KA = torch.empty_like(KA)
302	        d_KB = torch.empty_like(KB)
303	        d_VA = torch.empty_like(VA)
304	        d_VB = torch.empty_like(VB)
305	
306	        # Q Projection
307	        # d_QA = X.t() @ (dQ @ QB.t())
308	        # d_QB = (QA.t() @ X.t()) @ dQ
309	        # d_QA *= QS
310	        # d_QB *= QS
311	        d_QA.addmm_(X.t(), dQ @ QB.t(), alpha = QS, beta = 0)
312	        d_QB.addmm_(QA.t() @ X.t(), dQ, alpha = QS, beta = 0)
313	
314	        # K Projection
315	        # d_KA = X.t() @ (dK @ KB.t())
316	        # d_KB = (KA.t() @ X.t()) @ dK
317	        # d_KA *= KS
318	        # d_KB *= KS
319	        d_KA.addmm_(X.t(), dK @ KB.t(), alpha = KS, beta = 0)
320	        d_KB.addmm_(KA.t() @ X.t(), dK, alpha = KS, beta = 0)
321	
322	        # V Projection
323	        # d_VA = X.t() @ (dV @ VB.t())
324	        # d_VB = (VA.t() @ X.t()) @ dV
325	        # d_VA *= VS
326	        # d_VB *= VS
327	        d_VA.addmm_(X.t(), dV @ VB.t(), alpha = VS, beta = 0)
328	        d_VB.addmm_(VA.t() @ X.t(), dV, alpha = VS, beta = 0)
329	
330	        # Combine derivatives to find dX
331	        # dQ
332	        QW = fast_dequantize(QW.t(), QW_quant)
333	        dX = torch.matmul(dQ, QW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
334	        del QW
335	        # dX += (dQ @ QB.to(dtype).t() @ (QS * QA.to(dtype).t()))
336	        dX.addmm_(dQ @ QB.t(), QA.t(), alpha = QS)
337	
338	        # dK
339	        KW = fast_dequantize(KW.t(), KW_quant)
340	        # dX += dK @ KW.t()
341	        dX.addmm_(dK, KW.t())
342	        del KW
343	        # dX += dK @ KB.to(dtype).t() @ (KS * KA.to(dtype).t())
344	        dX.addmm_(dK @ KB.t(), KA.t(), alpha = KS)
345	
346	        # dV
347	        VW = fast_dequantize(VW.t(), VW_quant)
348	        # dX += dV @ VW.t()
349	        dX.addmm_(dV, VW.t())
350	        del VW
351	        # dX += dV @ VB.to(dtype).t() @ (VS * VA.to(dtype).t())
352	        dX.addmm_(dV @ VB.t(), VA.t(), alpha = VS)
353	
354	        # QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
355	        # KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
356	        # VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
357	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
358	            None, None, d_QA.t(), d_QB.t(), None, \
359	            None, None, d_KA.t(), d_KB.t(), None, \
360	            None, None, d_VA.t(), d_VB.t(), None, \
361	            None,
362	    pass
363	pass

381	class LoRA_W(torch.autograd.Function):
382	    """
383	    ### LoRA weights
384	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
385	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
386	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
387	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
388	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
389	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
390	
391	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
392	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
393	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
394	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
395	
396	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
397	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
398	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
399	
400	    ### K projection LoRA weights
401	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
402	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
403	
404	    ### V projection LoRA weights
405	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
406	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
407	    """
408	    @staticmethod
409	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
410	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
411	                W, W_quant, A, B, S):
412	        dtype = X.dtype
413	        XW = matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, S)
414	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (W, W_quant, S,)
415	        ctx.save_for_backward(A, B, X)
416	        return XW
417	    pass
418	
419	    @staticmethod
420	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
421	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
422	        W, W_quant, S = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
423	        A, B, X = ctx.saved_tensors
424	
425	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
426	        dY = dY.reshape(-1, dY.shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
427	        X  = X .reshape(-1, X .shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
428	        dtype = X.dtype
429	
430	        A, B = A.to(dtype), B.to(dtype)
431	
432	        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
433	
434	        d_A = torch.empty_like(A)
435	        d_B = torch.empty_like(B)
436	
437	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
438	        # Weight projection
439	        # d_A = X.t() @ (dY @ B.t())
440	        # d_B = (A.t() @ X.t()) @ dY
441	        # d_A *= S
442	        # d_B *= S
443	        d_A.addmm_(X.t(), dY @ B.t(), alpha = S, beta = 0)
444	        d_B.addmm_(A.t() @ X.t(), dY, alpha = S, beta = 0)
445	
446	        # Get derivative for dX
447	        W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant)
448	        dX = dY @ W.t()
449	        del W
450	        # dX += dY @ B.to(dtype).t() @ (S * A.to(dtype).t())
451	        dX.addmm_(dY @ B.t(), A.t(), alpha = S)
452	
453	        # W, W_quant, A, B, S
454	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
455	            None, None, d_A.t(), d_B.t(), None
456	    pass
457	pass

468	IDENTITY_DROPOUT = torch.nn.Identity
469	@torch._disable_dynamo
470	def fast_lora_forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, *args, **kwargs) -> torch.Tensor:
471	    raise NotImplementedError(
472	        "Unsloth: Currently not supported yet - reshaping done incorrectly"
473	    )
474	    self._check_forward_args(x, *args, **kwargs)
475	    adapter_names = kwargs.pop("adapter_names", None)
476	
477	    if self.disable_adapters:
478	        if self.merged:
479	            self.unmerge()
480	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
481	    elif adapter_names is not None:
482	        result = self._mixed_batch_forward(x, *args, adapter_names=adapter_names, **kwargs)
483	    elif self.merged:
484	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
485	    else:
486	        # Fastpath
487	        if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
488	            active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
489	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys(): return self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
490	
491	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
492	            if isinstance(dropout, IDENTITY_DROPOUT) and not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
493	                lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
494	                lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
495	                scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
496	                W = self.base_layer.weight
497	                return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
498	            pass
499	        pass
500	
501	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
502	        # As per Tim Dettmers, for 4bit, we need to defensively clone here.
503	        # The reason is that in some cases, an error can occur that backprop
504	        # does not work on a manipulated view. This issue may be solved with
505	        # newer PyTorch versions but this would need extensive testing to be
506	        # sure.
507	        result = result.clone()
508	
509	        for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
510	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
511	                continue
512	            lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
513	            lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
514	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
515	            scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
516	
517	            requires_conversion = not torch.is_autocast_enabled()
518	            if requires_conversion:
519	                expected_dtype = result.dtype
520	                x = x.to(lora_A.weight.dtype)
521	
522	            if not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
523	                result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
524	            else:
525	                if isinstance(dropout, torch.nn.Identity) or not self.training:
526	                    base_result = result
527	                else:
528	                    x = dropout(x)
529	                    base_result = None
530	
531	                result = result + self.lora_magnitude_vector[active_adapter](
532	                    x,
533	                    lora_A=lora_A,
534	                    lora_B=lora_B,
535	                    scaling=scaling,
536	                    base_layer=self.get_base_layer(),
537	                    base_result=base_result,
538	                )
539	            if requires_conversion:
540	                result = result.to(expected_dtype)
541	
542	    return result
543	pass

1788	    """

1793	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1794	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1795	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1796	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1797	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1798	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1799	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1800	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
1801	        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
1802	        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1803	        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1804	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
1805	        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
1806	        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
1807	        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
1808	        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
1809	        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
1810	        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
1811	        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
1812	        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
1813	        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
1814	        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
1815	        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
1816	        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
1817	        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
1818	    """
1819	    if tokenizer is None:
1820	        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")
1821	
1822	    arguments = dict(locals())

1824	    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
1825	    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
1826	    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
1827	    del arguments["self"]
1828	    del arguments["quantization_method"]
1829	    del arguments["first_conversion"]
1830	
1831	    # Fix tokenizer adding an extra BOS token at the front
1832	    fix_bos_token, old_chat_template = fix_tokenizer_bos_token(tokenizer)
1833

1841	            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)
1842	            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
1843	            makefile = None
1844	        else:
1845	            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
1846	            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
1847	            git_clone.wait()
1848	            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
1849	            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
1850	            python_install.wait()
1851	        pass

205	def unsloth_save_model(
206	    model,
207	    tokenizer,
208	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
209	    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
210	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
211	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
212	    is_main_process      : bool = True,
213	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
214	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
215	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
216	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
217	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
218	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
219	
220	    # Push to hub
221	    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
222	    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
223	    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
224	    create_pr            : bool = False,
225	    revision             : str = None,
226	    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
227	    tags                 : List[str] = None,
228	
229	    # Our functions
230	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
231	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
232	):
233	    if token is None: token = get_token()
234	
235	    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
236	    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
237	        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
238	    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()
239	
240	    if commit_description is None:
241	        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
242	    elif "Unsloth 2x faster" not in commit_description:
243	        commit_description += " (Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
244	    pass
245	
246	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
247	        raise RuntimeError(
248	            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
249	            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
250	            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
251	            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
252	        )
253	    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
254	        save_method = "merged_4bit"
255	    pass
256	
257	    save_pretrained_settings = dict(locals())
258	    for deletion in ("model", "tokenizer", "save_method", "temporary_location", "maximum_memory_usage"):
259	        del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
260	    pass
261	
262	    # First check for a token!
263	    if push_to_hub:
264	        from huggingface_hub import whoami
265	        try:
266	            username = whoami(token = token)["name"]
267	        except:
268	            raise RuntimeError(
269	                "Unsloth: Please supply a token!\n"\
270	                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens"
271	            )
272	        pass
273	    pass
274	
275	    assert(maximum_memory_usage > 0 and maximum_memory_usage <= 0.95)
276	
277	    # Clean memory up first
278	    for _ in range(3):
279	        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
280	        gc.collect()
281	    pass
282	
283	    save_method = save_method.lower().replace(" ", "_")
284	    if save_method != "lora" and save_method != "merged_16bit" and save_method != "merged_4bit":
285	        raise RuntimeError(
286	            "Unsloth: You must select one of 3 options when saving models:\n"\
287	            '"lora"         ==> This is the fastest and easiet. Just saves LoRA modules.\n'\
288	            '"merged_16bit" ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to float16. Needed for llama.cpp / GGUF.\n'\
289	            '"merged_4bit"  ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to 4bit. Useful for DPO / inference.'
290	        )
291	    pass
292	
293	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
294	
295	        print("Unsloth: Merging 4bit and LoRA weights to 4bit...")
296	        print("This might take 5 minutes...")
297	
298	        # Counteract no LoRA adapters!
299	        if hasattr(model, "merge_and_unload"):
300	            model = model.merge_and_unload()
301	        pass
302	        print("Done.")
303	    pass
304	
305	    if tags is not None:
306	        assert(isinstance(tags, (list, tuple)))
307	        tags = list(tags) + ["unsloth",]
308	    else:
309	        tags = ["unsloth",]
310	    pass
311	    save_pretrained_settings["tags"] = tags
312	
313	    if ((save_method == "lora") or (save_method == "merged_4bit")) and push_to_hub:
314	        if token is None:
315	            raise RuntimeError(
316	                "Unsloth: Pushing to HF requires a token. Pass `token = 'hf_....'`\n"\
317	                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens."
318	            )
319	        pass
320	
321	        if save_method == "lora":
322	            print("Unsloth: Saving LoRA adapters. Please wait...")
323	        elif save_method == "merged_4bit":

325	        pass
326

331	            old_username = None, private = private,
332	        )
333	
334	        getattr(model, "original_push_to_hub", model.push_to_hub)\
335	        (
336	            repo_id            = save_directory,
337	            use_temp_dir       = use_temp_dir,
338	            commit_message     = commit_message,

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )

261	    """

269	        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
270	    return X

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:

229	def convert_weights_back_to_dtype(model, dtype):
230	    """
231	    SFTTrainer calls get_peft_model and prepare_model_for_kbit_training which converts all weights to float32.
232	    This function converts the non-loraweights back to the original dtype.
233	    """
234	    for name, param in model.named_parameters():
235	        if any(s in name for s in ["norm", "embed"]):
236	            param.data = param.data.to(dtype)

239	def fix_llama3_tokenizer(tokenizer, padding_side="right"):
240	    tokenizer.padding_side = padding_side
241	    added_vocab = tokenizer.get_added_vocab()
242	    pad_token = [w for w in added_vocab if "pad" in w]
243	    assert len(pad_token) == 1
244	    tokenizer.pad_token = pad_token[0]  # Load dataset from the hub
245	    return tokenizer

248	def replace_module(
249	    module: torch.nn.Module,
250	    target_module_type: torch.nn.Module,
251	    conversion_func: Callable,
252	):
253	    for child_name, child_module in module.named_children():
254	        if isinstance(child_module, target_module_type):
255	            new_module = conversion_func(child_module)
256	            setattr(module, child_name, new_module)
257	        else:
258	            replace_module(child_module, target_module_type, conversion_func)
259	
260	
261	def _convert_lora_to_linear(module: LoraLayer, adapter_name: str = "default"):
262	    base_layer = module.get_base_layer()
263	    weight = base_layer.weight
264	
265	    assert isinstance(weight, bnb.nn.Params4bit)
266	    quant_state = weight.quant_state
267	    original_dtype = quant_state.dtype
268	
269	    w_dq = dequantize_4bit(weight.data, quant_state).float()
270	    lora_delta = (
271	        module.lora_B[adapter_name].weight
272	        @ module.lora_A[adapter_name].weight
273	        * module.scaling[adapter_name]
274	    )
275	    w_dq += lora_delta.float()
276	    w_dq = w_dq.to(original_dtype)
277	
278	    new_module = torch.nn.Linear(
279	        w_dq.shape[1], w_dq.shape[0], bias=module.base_layer.bias is not None
280	    )
281	    new_module.weight.data = torch.nn.Parameter(w_dq, requires_grad=False)
282	    if module.lora_bias[adapter_name]:
283	        bias_data = module.base_layer.bias.data + module.lora_B[adapter_name].bias
284	        new_module.bias.data = torch.nn.Parameter(bias_data, requires_grad=False)
285	    return new_module
286	
287	
288	def convert_lora_to_linear(model: torch.nn.Module):
289	    replace_module(model, LoraLayer, _convert_lora_to_linear)
290	    assert not any(isinstance(module, LoraLayer) for module in model.modules())
291	    return model

1788	    """
1789	        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto

1791	
1792	        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
1793	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1794	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1795	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1796	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1797	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1798	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1799	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1800	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
1801	        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
1802	        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1803	        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1804	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
1805	        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
1806	        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
1807	        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
1808	        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
1809	        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
1810	        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
1811	        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
1812	        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
1813	        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
1814	        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
1815	        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
1816	        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
1817	        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
1818	    """
1819	    if tokenizer is None:
1820	        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")

1822	    arguments = dict(locals())
1823	    arguments["model"]        = self
1824	    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
1825	    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
1826	    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
1827	    del arguments["self"]
1828	    del arguments["quantization_method"]
1829	    del arguments["first_conversion"]

This is the core mechanism for dynamically attaching methods to model instances:

```python

model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
model.push_to_hub_ggml = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
model.save_pretrained_ggml = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
```

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive report:

## Summary: LoRA Adapter Weights Storage and Access in Unsloth

Based on my exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's how LoRA adapter weights are stored and accessed:

### 1. **How to Access lora_A and lora_B Weight Matrices**

LoRA weights are stored within PEFT's `LoraLayer` objects (from `peft.tuners.lora.layer`). Access pattern:

```python
# From get_lora_parameters() in /unsloth/kernels/utils.py (lines 214-219)
adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)[0]  # Get adapter name (typically "default")

lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]       # Returns a Linear module
lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]       # Returns a Linear module

# Access actual weight tensors
A = lora_A_linear.weight                   # Shape: (r, input_dim)
B = lora_B_linear.weight                   # Shape: (output_dim, r)
```

**Key details:**
- `lora_A` and `lora_B` are dictionaries keyed by adapter name
- Each entry is a `torch.nn.Linear` module
- The `.weight` attribute of these modules contains the actual tensors
- They follow the low-rank factorization: `output = base_output + (input @ A.T @ B.T) * scaling`

**File reference:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` lines 190-232

---

### 2. **Data Structure and Tensor Shapes**

**Projection structure:**
```
layer (e.g., q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj)
├── base_layer: Original linear layer
│   └── weight: shape (output_dim, input_dim)
├── lora_A: dict[str, Linear]
│   └── [adapter_name].weight: shape (r, input_dim)
├── lora_B: dict[str, Linear]
│   └── [adapter_name].weight: shape (output_dim, r)
├── scaling: dict[str, float]
│   └── [adapter_name]: lora_alpha / r
├── lora_dropout: dict[str, Dropout]
└── active_adapters: list[str]
```

**Example from fast_lora.py (lines 493-495):**
```python
lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight      # (r, in_features)
lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight      # (out_features, r)
scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]           # scalar = lora_alpha / r
```

---

### 3. **Rank (r), Alpha, and Target Modules Metadata**

**Storage locations:**

1. **Rank (r) and Alpha:**
   - Stored in `model.peft_config["default"]` (LoraConfig object)
   - Access pattern:
   ```python
   peft_config = model.peft_config["default"].to_dict()
   r = peft_config["r"]                    # LoRA rank
   lora_alpha = peft_config["lora_alpha"]  # Alpha parameter
   ```
   - Scaling factor computed as: `scaling = lora_alpha / r` and stored per-adapter in `proj.scaling[adapter]`

2. **Target Modules:**
   - Also in peft_config:
   ```python
   target_modules = peft_config["target_modules"]  # List of module names
   # e.g., ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"]
   ```

**File references:**
- `/unsloth/models/llama.py` lines 2336, 2346, 2598-2617
- `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` lines 225-231 (scaling storage)

---

### 4. **PEFT Integration - Custom vs PEFT Implementation**

**Unsloth uses PEFT's standard LoraLayer:**

```python
# From /unsloth/models/_utils.py lines 845-864
from peft.tuners.lora.layer import LoraLayer
```

**However, Unsloth applies optimizations:**

1. **Fast LoRA forwards:** Uses custom CUDA kernels for efficient computation
   - File: `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`
   - Patches `peft.tuners.lora.bnb.Linear4bit.forward` with `fast_lora_forward`
   - Function: `patch_fast_lora()` in `/unsloth/models/_utils.py` lines 1412-1415

2. **Custom forward implementations:**
   - `LoRA_MLP` autograd function (lines 28-174)
   - `LoRA_QKV` autograd function (lines 224-363)
   - `LoRA_W` autograd function (lines 381-457)

3. **Computation uses PEFT's base layer:**
   - Gets base_layer from projection via `getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)`
   - Accesses `proj.lora_A[adapter]`, `proj.lora_B[adapter]`, `proj.scaling[adapter]`

**File references:**
- `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` - Custom efficient implementations
- `/unsloth/models/_utils.py` lines 843-871 - PEFT version compatibility patches

---

### 5. **How to Iterate Over LoRA Layers in a Model**

**Method 1: Iterate via model.modules()**
```python
from peft.tuners.lora.layer import LoraLayer

for module in model.modules():
    if isinstance(module, LoraLayer):
        # Access LoRA weights
        adapter_name = module.active_adapters[0]
        lora_A = module.lora_A[adapter_name].weight
        lora_B = module.lora_B[adapter_name].weight
        scaling = module.scaling[adapter_name]
```

**Method 2: Via named_modules()**
```python
for name, module in model.named_modules():
    if hasattr(module, 'lora_A'):  # Check if it's a LoRA layer
        # name will be something like "model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj"
        adapter_name = module.active_adapters[0]
        # ... access lora_A, lora_B, scaling
```

**Method 3: Via state_dict() - See merged weights**
```python
state_dict = model.state_dict()
for key, value in state_dict.items():
    if 'lora_' in key:
        # e.g., "model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj.lora_A.default.weight"
        print(key, value.shape)
```

**File reference:** `/tests/utils/hf_utils.py` lines 261-290 shows iteration patterns

---

### 6. **How state_dict() and named_modules() Are Used**

**During model saving/merging** (`/unsloth/save.py` lines 159-187):
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    # Uses get_lora_parameters_bias to extract weights
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
    
    if A is not None:
        # Merge: W += scaling * A.T @ B.T
        W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
    
    return W, bias
```

**During state_dict iteration for layer merging** (`/unsloth/save.py` lines 551-609):
- Iterates through model layers
- For each LoRA-enabled projection, calls `_merge_lora()` to compute merged weights
- Reconstructs state_dict with merged weights only

**Getting LoRA parameters helper** (`/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` lines 190-232):
```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """Return 5-tuple: (weight, quant_state, lora_A, lora_B, scaling)"""
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)[0]
    
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    
    return (W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), 
            lora_A_linear.weight, lora_B_linear.weight, 
            proj.scaling[adapter])
```

---

### 7. **Configuration Access Patterns**

```python
# Access PEFT config
peft_config = model.peft_config["default"]  # or model.peft_config["default"].to_dict()

# Key fields:
config_dict = {
    "r": 16,                                    # Rank
    "lora_alpha": 16,                          # Alpha
    "target_modules": ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj"],  # List
    "lora_dropout": 0.0,
    "bias": "none",
    "layers_to_transform": None,
    "init_lora_weights": True,
    "loftq_config": None,
    "use_rslora": False,
}
```

**File reference:** `/unsloth/models/llama.py` lines 2334-2365 (config validation)

---

### Summary Table

| Component | Storage Location | Type | Access Method |
|-----------|------------------|------|----------------|
| lora_A | `module.lora_A[adapter_name]` | `torch.nn.Linear` | `.weight` attribute |
| lora_B | `module.lora_B[adapter_name]` | `torch.nn.Linear` | `.weight` attribute |
| scaling | `module.scaling[adapter_name]` | `float` | Direct dict access |
| rank (r) | `model.peft_config["default"].r` | `int` | Config attribute |
| alpha | `model.peft_config["default"].lora_alpha` | `int` | Config attribute |
| target_modules | `model.peft_config["default"].target_modules` | `list[str]` | Config attribute |

---

**Key File References for Implementation Details:**
- `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` - Core parameter access functions
- `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` - Fast forward/backward implementations
- `/unsloth/models/llama.py` - LoRA configuration and setup
- `/unsloth/save.py` - LoRA merging and weight access during saving
- `/tests/utils/hf_utils.py` - Example iteration and conversion patterns

```
├── base_layer: Original linear layer
│   └── weight: shape (output_dim, input_dim)
├── lora_A: dict[str, Linear]
│   └── [adapter_name].weight: shape (r, input_dim)
├── lora_B: dict[str, Linear]
│   └── [adapter_name].weight: shape (output_dim, r)
├── scaling: dict[str, float]
│   └── [adapter_name]: lora_alpha / r
├── lora_dropout: dict[str, Dropout]
└── active_adapters: list[str]
```

**Example from fast_lora.py (lines 493-495):**
```python
lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight      # (r, in_features)
lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight      # (out_features, r)
scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]           # scalar = lora_alpha / r
```

### 3. **Rank (r), Alpha, and Target Modules Metadata**

**Storage locations:**

1. **Rank (r) and Alpha:**
   - Stored in `model.peft_config["default"]` (LoraConfig object)
   - Access pattern:
   ```python
   peft_config = model.peft_config["default"].to_dict()
   r = peft_config["r"]                    # LoRA rank
   lora_alpha = peft_config["lora_alpha"]  # Alpha parameter
   ```
   - Scaling factor computed as: `scaling = lora_alpha / r` and stored per-adapter in `proj.scaling[adapter]`

2. **Target Modules:**
   - Also in peft_config:
   ```python
   target_modules = peft_config["target_modules"]  # List of module names
   # e.g., ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"]
   ```

**File references:**
- `/unsloth/models/llama.py` lines 2336, 2346, 2598-2617
- `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` lines 225-231 (scaling storage)

### 4. **PEFT Integration - Custom vs PEFT Implementation**

**Unsloth uses PEFT's standard LoraLayer:**

```python
# From /unsloth/models/_utils.py lines 845-864
from peft.tuners.lora.layer import LoraLayer
```

**However, Unsloth applies optimizations:**

1. **Fast LoRA forwards:** Uses custom CUDA kernels for efficient computation
   - File: `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`
   - Patches `peft.tuners.lora.bnb.Linear4bit.forward` with `fast_lora_forward`
   - Function: `patch_fast_lora()` in `/unsloth/models/_utils.py` lines 1412-1415

2. **Custom forward implementations:**
   - `LoRA_MLP` autograd function (lines 28-174)
   - `LoRA_QKV` autograd function (lines 224-363)
   - `LoRA_W` autograd function (lines 381-457)

3. **Computation uses PEFT's base layer:**
   - Gets base_layer from projection via `getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)`
   - Accesses `proj.lora_A[adapter]`, `proj.lora_B[adapter]`, `proj.scaling[adapter]`

- `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` - Custom efficient implementations
- `/unsloth/models/_utils.py` lines 843-871 - PEFT version compatibility patches

### 5. **How to Iterate Over LoRA Layers in a Model**

**Method 1: Iterate via model.modules()**
```python
from peft.tuners.lora.layer import LoraLayer

for module in model.modules():
    if isinstance(module, LoraLayer):
        # Access LoRA weights
        adapter_name = module.active_adapters[0]
        lora_A = module.lora_A[adapter_name].weight
        lora_B = module.lora_B[adapter_name].weight
        scaling = module.scaling[adapter_name]
```

```python
        adapter_name = module.active_adapters[0]
        # ... access lora_A, lora_B, scaling
```

### 6. **How state_dict() and named_modules() Are Used**

**During model saving/merging** (`/unsloth/save.py` lines 159-187):
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    # Uses get_lora_parameters_bias to extract weights
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
    
    if A is not None:
        # Merge: W += scaling * A.T @ B.T
        W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
    
    return W, bias
```

**During state_dict iteration for layer merging** (`/unsloth/save.py` lines 551-609):
- Iterates through model layers
- For each LoRA-enabled projection, calls `_merge_lora()` to compute merged weights
- Reconstructs state_dict with merged weights only

**Getting LoRA parameters helper** (`/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` lines 190-232):
```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """Return 5-tuple: (weight, quant_state, lora_A, lora_B, scaling)"""
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)[0]
    
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    
    return (W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), 
            lora_A_linear.weight, lora_B_linear.weight, 
            proj.scaling[adapter])
```

### 4. **How LoRA Adapters Are Stored and Accessed**

#### **LoRA Weight Structure**

LoRA weights are accessed through the `get_lora_parameters_bias()` function in `/kernels/utils.py:235`:

```python
def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    """
    Extracts LoRA parameters from a projection layer.
    Returns: (W, quant_state, lora_A, lora_B, scaling, bias)
    """
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # If adapters disabled or merged, return base weight only
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias

    # Get active adapter name
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    # Extract LoRA parameters
    return (
        W,                              # Base weight
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),# Quantization state (if 4bit)
        proj.lora_A[adapter].weight,    # LoRA A matrix (low rank)
        proj.lora_B[adapter].weight,    # LoRA B matrix (low rank)
        proj.scaling[adapter],          # LoRA scaling factor
        base_layer.bias,                # Bias term
    )
```

#### **LoRA Configuration Parameters**

Used in `get_peft_model()` at `/models/llama.py:2266`:

```python
def get_peft_model(
    model,
    r = 16,                           # LoRA rank
    target_modules = ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
                      "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"],
    lora_alpha = 16,                  # Scaling factor
    lora_dropout = 0.0,               # Dropout in LoRA layers
    bias = "none",                    # Bias handling
    layers_to_transform = None,       # Specific layers to apply LoRA
    layers_pattern = None,            # Pattern for layer selection
    use_rslora = False,               # Rank-Stabilized LoRA
    modules_to_save = None,           # Modules to save without LoRA
    init_lora_weights = True,         # Initialize LoRA weights
    loftq_config = {},                # LoFTQ initialization config
    **kwargs,
)
```

#### **Attribute Access Paths**

Once LoRA is applied, these attributes become available on projection layers:
- `proj.lora_A[adapter_name]` - LoRA A matrices (dict of adapters)
- `proj.lora_B[adapter_name]` - LoRA B matrices (dict of adapters)
- `proj.scaling[adapter_name]` - Scaling factors (dict of adapters)
- `proj.base_layer` - Access to base layer (for wrapped modules)
- `proj.merged` - Boolean indicating if LoRA has been merged
- `proj.disable_adapters` - Boolean to disable adapters
- `proj.active_adapters()` - Method to get list of active adapters

### 5. **Main Public API Entry Points**

#### **Loading Models**
```python
# Language models
model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(
    model_name="unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct",
    max_seq_length=2048,
    dtype=None,
    load_in_4bit=True,  # 4-bit QLoRA
    load_in_8bit=False,
    full_finetuning=False,
    token=None,
    device_map="sequential",
    ...
)

# Vision models
model, tokenizer = FastVisionModel.from_pretrained(...)

# More flexible loading
model, tokenizer = FastModel.from_pretrained(...)
```

#### **Applying LoRA**
```python
model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(
    model,
    r=16,
    target_modules=["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj"],
    lora_alpha=16,
    lora_dropout=0.0,
    use_gradient_checkpointing="unsloth",
)
```

#### **Saving Models**
```python
# Save LoRA adapters only
model.save_pretrained("path/to/lora")
model.push_to_hub("repo_id")

# Save merged 16-bit model (for GGUF conversion)
model.save_pretrained_merged("path/to/merged", save_method="merged_16bit")
model.push_to_hub_merged("repo_id", save_method="merged_16bit")

# Convert to GGUF format (llama.cpp compatible)
model.save_pretrained_gguf(
    "path/to/gguf",
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    quantization_method="q4_k_m"  # or "fast_quantized", "quantized"
)
model.push_to_hub_gguf("repo_id", tokenizer=tokenizer)

# Convert LoRA to GGML format
model.save_pretrained_ggml("path/to/ggml")

# Save with TorchAO quantization
model.save_pretrained_torchao("path/to/torchao", torchao_config=None)
```

### 8. **Configuration and Quantization Constants**

**Supported GGUF quantization methods** (from `/save.py:79-100`):
- `q4_k_m`, `q4_k_s` - Recommended 4-bit
- `q5_k_m`, `q5_k_s` - Recommended 5-bit  
- `q2_k`, `q3_k_*`, `q6_k` - Various alternatives
- `q8_0` - Fast 8-bit
- `f16`, `bf16`, `f32` - Full precision variants
- `iq2_xxs`, `iq2_xs`, `iq3_xxs` - I-quantization methods

**Save methods**:
- `"lora"` - LoRA adapters only
- `"merged_16bit"` - Merge to float16 (for GGUF)
- `"merged_4bit"` - Merge to int4 (for inference)

---

### Summary

Unsloth is a highly optimized fine-tuning library focused on **speed and memory efficiency**. The architecture centers around:

1. **Model patching** - Replaces forward passes with optimized CUDA kernels
2. **LoRA support** - Uses PEFT library, with custom kernel optimizations
3. **Multiple export formats** - GGUF (llama.cpp), standard HF, GGML
4. **4-bit quantization** - Integrates with bitsandbytes for QLoRA
5. **No ONNX support** - Limited to above formats

The codebase is well-structured with clear separation between model loading, training optimization, and export functionality.

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me compile my findings:

## Unsloth Codebase Exploration - Comprehensive Report

### 1. **Top-Level Directory Layout**

```
/unsloth/
├── __init__.py                    # Main entry point, imports core functionality
├── _auto_install.py               # Auto-installation utilities
├── import_fixes.py                # Fixes for various import-related issues
├── save.py                        # Core save/export functionality (105KB)
├── chat_templates.py              # Chat template definitions
├── trainer.py                     # Training utilities
├── tokenizer_utils.py             # Tokenizer-related utilities
├── models/                        # Model implementations
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── loader.py                  # FastLanguageModel, FastModel, FastVisionModel
│   ├── loader_utils.py
│   ├── _utils.py                  # Utilities for model patches and setup
│   ├── llama.py                   # FastLlamaModel (main base class)
│   ├── mistral.py                 # FastMistralModel
│   ├── qwen2.py, qwen3.py, qwen3_moe.py
│   ├── gemma.py, gemma2.py
│   ├── granite.py, falcon_h1.py
│   ├── cohere.py
│   ├── dpo.py                     # DPO trainer patching
│   ├── rl.py, rl_replacements.py # Reinforcement learning
│   ├── vision.py                  # FastBaseModel (vision models)
│   └── mapper.py                  # Model mapping utilities
├── kernels/                       # CUDA kernels
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── fast_lora.py              # LoRA kernels
│   ├── utils.py                   # Contains get_lora_parameters_bias()
│   ├── layernorm.py, rms_layernorm.py
│   ├── rope_embedding.py
│   ├── cross_entropy_loss.py
│   ├── flex_attention.py
│   └── moe/                       # Mixture of Experts kernels
├── registry/                      # Model registry
│   ├── registry.py
│   ├── _llama.py, _mistral.py, _qwen.py, etc.
│   └── REGISTRY.md
├── dataprep/                      # Data preparation
└── utils/
    └── hf_hub.py                  # Hugging Face hub utilities
```

---

### 2. **Main Model Classes and Their File Locations**

| Class | File | Inheritance | Purpose |
|-------|------|-----------|---------|
| **FastLlamaModel** | `/models/llama.py:1778` | Base for Llama-based models | Core model patcher with rope scaling, attention, and forward passes |
| **FastLanguageModel** | `/models/loader.py:107` | Extends FastLlamaModel | Public API entry point for language models |
| **FastModel** | `/models/loader.py:526` | Extends FastBaseModel | Handles 8bit/16bit loading and full finetuning |
| **FastVisionModel** | `/models/loader.py:995` | Extends FastModel | Vision-language models |
| **FastTextModel** | `/models/loader.py:998` | Extends FastModel | Text-only models |
| **FastBaseModel** | `/models/vision.py:283` | Standalone | Base for all model types |
| **FastMistralModel** | `/models/mistral.py:394` | Extends FastLlamaModel | Mistral-specific optimizations |
| **FastGemmaModel** | `/models/gemma.py:336` | Extends FastLlamaModel | Gemma-specific optimizations |
| **FastQwen2Model** | `/models/qwen2.py:38` | Extends FastLlamaModel | Qwen2-specific optimizations |
| **FastQwen3Model** | `/models/qwen3.py:387` | Extends FastLlamaModel | Qwen3-specific optimizations |
| **FastGraniteModel** | `/models/granite.py:456` | Extends FastLlamaModel | Granite-specific optimizations |

---

### 3. **All Existing Save and Export Methods**

#### **A. Main User-Facing Save Methods (added via `patch_saving_functions`)**

Located in `/save.py` (lines 2569-2678), these methods are dynamically attached to model instances:

```python
# Core save methods
model.push_to_hub_merged()           # Push merged model to HF Hub
model.save_pretrained_merged()       # Save merged model locally
model.push_to_hub_gguf()            # Push GGUF format to HF Hub
model.save_pretrained_gguf()        # Save GGUF format locally
model.push_to_hub_ggml()            # Push GGML format to HF Hub
model.save_pretrained_ggml()        # Save GGML format locally
model.save_pretrained_torchao()     # Save with TorchAO quantization
model.push_to_hub()                 # Standard HF push (patched wrapper)
```

#### **B. Function Signatures**

**1. `unsloth_save_model()` - Main unified save function**
```python
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method: str = "lora",  # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process: bool = True,
    state_dict: Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function: Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size: Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization: bool = True,
    variant: Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format: bool = True,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags: List[str] = None,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.9,
)
# Location: /save.py:205-763
```

**2. `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` - GGUF conversion and save**

---

### 4. **How LoRA Adapters Are Stored and Accessed**

#### **LoRA Weight Structure**

LoRA weights are accessed through the `get_lora_parameters_bias()` function in `/kernels/utils.py:235`:

```python
def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    """
    Extracts LoRA parameters from a projection layer.
    Returns: (W, quant_state, lora_A, lora_B, scaling, bias)
    """
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # If adapters disabled or merged, return base weight only
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias

    # Get active adapter name
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    # Extract LoRA parameters
    return (
        W,                              # Base weight
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),# Quantization state (if 4bit)
        proj.lora_A[adapter].weight,    # LoRA A matrix (low rank)
        proj.lora_B[adapter].weight,    # LoRA B matrix (low rank)
        proj.scaling[adapter],          # LoRA scaling factor
        base_layer.bias,                # Bias term
    )
```

#### **LoRA Configuration Parameters**

Used in `get_peft_model()` at `/models/llama.py:2266`:

```python
def get_peft_model(
    model,
    r = 16,                           # LoRA rank
    target_modules = ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
                      "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"],
    lora_alpha = 16,                  # Scaling factor
    lora_dropout = 0.0,               # Dropout in LoRA layers
    bias = "none",                    # Bias handling
    layers_to_transform = None,       # Specific layers to apply LoRA
    layers_pattern = None,            # Pattern for layer selection
    use_rslora = False,               # Rank-Stabilized LoRA
    modules_to_save = None,           # Modules to save without LoRA
    init_lora_weights = True,         # Initialize LoRA weights
    loftq_config = {},                # LoFTQ initialization config
    **kwargs,
)
```

#### **Attribute Access Paths**

Once LoRA is applied, these attributes become available on projection layers:
- `proj.lora_A[adapter_name]` - LoRA A matrices (dict of adapters)
- `proj.lora_B[adapter_name]` - LoRA B matrices (dict of adapters)
- `proj.scaling[adapter_name]` - Scaling factors (dict of adapters)
- `proj.base_layer` - Access to base layer (for wrapped modules)
- `proj.merged` - Boolean indicating if LoRA has been merged
- `proj.disable_adapters` - Boolean to disable adapters
- `proj.active_adapters()` - Method to get list of active adapters

---

### 5. **Main Public API Entry Points**

#### **Loading Models**
```python
# Language models
model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(
    model_name="unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct",
    max_seq_length=2048,
    dtype=None,
    load_in_4bit=True,  # 4-bit QLoRA
    load_in_8bit=False,
    full_finetuning=False,
    token=None,
    device_map="sequential",
    ...
)

# Vision models
model, tokenizer = FastVisionModel.from_pretrained(...)

# More flexible loading
model, tokenizer = FastModel.from_pretrained(...)
```

#### **Applying LoRA**
```python
model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(
    model,
    r=16,
    target_modules=["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj"],
    lora_alpha=16,
    lora_dropout=0.0,
    use_gradient_checkpointing="unsloth",
)
```

#### **Saving Models**
```python
# Save LoRA adapters only
model.save_pretrained("path/to/lora")
model.push_to_hub("repo_id")

# Save merged 16-bit model (for GGUF conversion)
model.save_pretrained_merged("path/to/merged", save_method="merged_16bit")
model.push_to_hub_merged("repo_id", save_method="merged_16bit")

# Convert to GGUF format (llama.cpp compatible)
model.save_pretrained_gguf(
    "path/to/gguf",
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    quantization_method="q4_k_m"  # or "fast_quantized", "quantized"
)
model.push_to_hub_gguf("repo_id", tokenizer=tokenizer)

# Convert LoRA to GGML format
model.save_pretrained_ggml("path/to/ggml")

# Save with TorchAO quantization
model.save_pretrained_torchao("path/to/torchao", torchao_config=None)
```

---

### 6. **ONNX Support**

**Status: NOT IMPLEMENTED**

A comprehensive search found **zero ONNX-related code** in the Unsloth repository:
- No ONNX import statements
- No ONNX export functions
- No ONNX optimization code
- No references in save functions

The codebase focuses on:
- **GGUF format** (llama.cpp) - fully implemented
- **GGML format** - partially implemented
- **TorchAO quantization** - supported
- **Standard HF format** - supported (via PEFT/transformers)

---

### 7. **Key Data Structures and Files**

| File | Key Contents |
|------|--------------|
| `/save.py` | All save/export logic, 2679 lines |
| `/models/llama.py` | FastLlamaModel (2400+ lines), base for all text models |
| `/models/vision.py` | FastBaseModel, vision model support |
| `/models/loader.py` | FastLanguageModel, FastModel, API entry points |
| `/kernels/utils.py` | `get_lora_parameters_bias()`, `fast_dequantize()` |
| `/kernels/fast_lora.py` | Low-level LoRA kernel implementations |

---

### 8. **Configuration and Quantization Constants**

**Supported GGUF quantization methods** (from `/save.py:79-100`):
- `q4_k_m`, `q4_k_s` - Recommended 4-bit
- `q5_k_m`, `q5_k_s` - Recommended 5-bit  
- `q2_k`, `q3_k_*`, `q6_k` - Various alternatives
- `q8_0` - Fast 8-bit
- `f16`, `bf16`, `f32` - Full precision variants
- `iq2_xxs`, `iq2_xs`, `iq3_xxs` - I-quantization methods

**Save methods**:
- `"lora"` - LoRA adapters only
- `"merged_16bit"` - Merge to float16 (for GGUF)
- `"merged_4bit"` - Merge to int4 (for inference)

---

### Summary

Unsloth is a highly optimized fine-tuning library focused on **speed and memory efficiency**. The architecture centers around:

1. **Model patching** - Replaces forward passes with optimized CUDA kernels
2. **LoRA support** - Uses PEFT library, with custom kernel optimizations
3. **Multiple export formats** - GGUF (llama.cpp), standard HF, GGML
4. **4-bit quantization** - Integrates with bitsandbytes for QLoRA
5. **No ONNX support** - Limited to above formats

The codebase is well-structured with clear separation between model loading, training optimization, and export functionality.

def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

def save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory):
    # Create the custom directory if it doesn't exist
    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # Call the unsloth_save_model function with the custom directory
    unsloth_save_model(
        model,
        tokenizer,
        save_directory=save_directory,
        save_method="lora",
        push_to_hub=False,
    )

# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Convert LoRA to GGML format using Unsloth",
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    lora_directory_push = "lora-to-ggml-push"
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, lora_directory_push)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(lora_directory_push, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {lora_directory_push} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {lora_directory_push} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return

    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")

    print("Unsloth: Uploading GGML file to Hugging Face Hub...")
    username = upload_to_huggingface(
        self, repo_id, token,
        "GGML converted LoRA", "ggml", output_file, None, private,
    )
    link = f"{repo_id.lstrip('/')}"
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Converted LoRA to GGML and uploaded to https://huggingface.co/{link}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(
    self,
    save_directory: str, # Added parameter for the folder name
    tokenizer,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    # Use the provided save_directory for local saving
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, save_directory)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(save_directory, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {save_directory} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {save_directory} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")
pass
